channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: HBO’s ‘The Last of Us’ Casts Yara and Lev for Season 3
item.content:encoded:
HBO’s The Last of Us series has cast its Yara and Lev. Per Deadline, the sibling pair from the second Last of Us game will be played by Michelle Mao and Kyriana Kratter, respectively.

Kyriana Kratter plays Lev, a transgender teenager, and Michelle Mao plays their sister, Yara. Both were born into the Seraphite cult. Love would be banished for his gender identity, and Yara would eventually aid in his escape. Lev is also the partner of Abby Anderson, played in the series by Kaitlyn Dever.

Michelle Mao is known for her work in Bridgerton. Kyrianna Kratter, on the other hand, is known for roles in Disney’s Bunk’d and Star Wars: Skeleton Crew.

The Last of Us Season 3 May Be Its Final Season

Season 3 of HBO’s series is expected to premiere in 2027, and it might be the show’s final season. Season 2 wrapped up in 2025, but not before setting the stage for a story told from the perspective of the previously mentioned Abby Anderson.

One reason season 3 is expected to be the final installment of the video game adaptation is that, once this story unfolds, it will mark the end of the source material. The last game in the Last of Us series was released in 2020.

It was heavily hinted earlier this year that this show is nearing its end with the upcoming season. Learn more about that here. The showrunner of Last of Us is currently working on a Baldur’s Gate series. Learn more about that here.

How do you feel about the casting choice for Yara and Lev? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: Resident Evil Producer Comments on Franchise’s 30th Anniversary
item.content:encoded:
Jun Takeuchi, the executive producer of the Resident Evil series, has commented on the franchise’s 30th Anniversary. March 22, 1996, saw the launch of the first Resident Evil game. The series is also known as Biohazard in Japan.

Currently, the successful horror franchise is breaking records with its latest release, Resident Evil Requiem, which has become the fastest-selling game in the franchise.

Jun Takeuchi’s Message to Resident Evil Fans Worldwide

Jun Takeuchi, via Capcom‘s European page on Twitter, extended a message of gratitude towards RE’s dedicated fan base. In his message, he noted that Requiem has now reached over 6 million players worldwide. Also, he promised the team will continue to bring wonderful experiences to gamers in the future.

“To our beloved fans of the Resident Evil series, Today marks the 30th anniversary of Resident Evil. We want to extend our heartfelt gratitude to all the fans who have supported us along the way since the release of our first game on March 2241, 1996.

“Last month, we released Resident Evil Requiem. With the return to Raccoon City, the place where it all began, we combined the two hearts of the series—horror and action—and packed them both into one title. And now, Requiem has reached over six million players around the world. Thank you all so much.

“Our team will continue to work hard to bring all of you even more wonderful experiences in the future—and it would be an honor if you continued along with us on that journey.
Thank you again for your support.”

Learn more about the success of Requiem at this link. For more future Resi games, check out the latest news on the remake of Code Veronica.

What’s your favorite Resident Evil game? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: Peni Parker Confirmed for Marvel Tōkon: Fighting Souls
item.content:encoded:
Peni Parker has been revealed as a playable character in the upcoming fighting game, Marvel Tōkon: Fighting Souls. Her inclusion was revealed in a trailer for The Amazing Guardians. This team is comprised of Spider-Man, Ms. Marvel, Star-Lord, and the aforementioned young Parker.

The official trailer was released during the Arc World Tour 2025–2026 Finals in Korea. An official PlayStation blog, written by game producer Takeshi Yamanaka, teases that players will learn more about how this team comes together by playing Episode Mode.

More Details on New Additions to Marvel Tōkon: Fighting Souls

The PlayStation blog also highlights the “Promoter,” another new character featured in the trailer. Her real name is revealed to be Xirena Awhina, and she’s an Elder of the Universe. It is said that she travels the galaxy organizing massive entertainment events, such as the high-stakes tournament that unfolds throughout the game’s story mode. Further details will be revealed soon.

Also, they’ve announced that the latest build of the game features Automatic Team Name Generation. The game will select characters and create a fun team name that references each character’s lore.

“The name appears on screen and is even announced by a ring announcer. Try out different combinations to discover cool, surprising, or just plain fun team names.”

The game is slated for release on August 6. It will be playable on PlayStation 5 and PC. Insider Gaming will continue to report the latest updates on the game as the release date nears.

Are you looking forward to playing this game? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: NYT Strands #749 Hints and Answers for Today, March 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #749 for Sunday, March 22, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, March 21, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #749 are all terms that have become the generic way to describe a specific item, despite being a unique branding term at first. For example: Kleenex, Photoshop, and Coke.

General Strands Hints For March 21

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: A metal contraption that closes your pants; an alternative to buttons.
  • Hint 2: One of the brand-new matches in WWE 2K26 sees players trying to throw their opponent into these sanitation containment areas.
  • Hint 3: When you have a headache, this is the most common medicine you’ll ask for.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Strands #749 for March 22, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for March 22, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • GAME
  • GAMER
  • GAMES
  • PITS
  • LATE

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #749 On March 22, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • DUMPSTER
  • ZIPPER
  • THERMOS
  • ESCALATOR
  • ASPIRIN
  • SPANAGRAM: GENERICTERM

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #749?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on March 23 will be Strands Game #750.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
